diary: Sunny Monday felt like it was already cheering me on. I woke up in Redmond, let the dog out, and made coffee while the light coming through the kitchen blinds looked almost too bright for welding-day reality. The morning drive to the Interbay shipyard was smooth, and I kept thinking about how the Light Rail being down between Westlake and University District might’ve bumped traffic—still, I didn’t hit much trouble. At Boeing Plant 2, I spent most of the shift on fabrication and inspection checklists, the kind of work where you can’t afford to rush. Around lunch I ducked to The Pink Door in Belltown for something quick and cold. Later I grabbed shop supplies and coffee at QFC in Ballard, then wrapped up in Woodinville with tomorrow’s weld schedule lined up. The USA vs Wales match was on at 11am, and I could feel the city buzzing through the bars downtown. Tonight I stocked up at Whole Foods, cooked at home, practiced salsa a little, and set out a board game—then just let the dog and the quiet carry me. I feel okay, almost steady.
thought_process: I planned my day around the obvious friction points: the Light Rail closure could’ve made transit chaos, so I stayed in the car and focused on getting to Interbay early enough to avoid delays. At work, I kept to checklists and material checks because that’s where mistakes hide—especially with welding runs that depend on timing and prep. I picked up lunch near the shipyard to save time, and QFC in Ballard for supplies because it’s quick and predictable. The World Cup match being on at 11am made me notice more people moving with purpose downtown; it gave me a subtle upbeat rhythm even though I stayed locked into my tasks. My mood shifted from “get it done” to “breathe” once I wrapped up and headed home, and by evening I felt grounded—like I’d earned the quiet.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
